BotFans We hear you.
 
One thing we’ve always loved about the BattleBots community is that you’re not shy about telling us what you think : )
 
For the Pro League, we wanted to try out a different style of editing, adopting techniques and pacing familiar to a YouTube audience.
 
Whilst that approach achieved some of what we were hoping for (early ratings data confirms this), many of you felt we pushed it too far. We agree. There’s a better balance to be found, and that’s exactly what we’re working towards.
 
Because of the time lag in the editing schedule the changes will take a little while to percolate through, so please bear with us. Soon the fights will be a little less frenetic and key backstage moments will be allowed to breathe. We need a better balance between what works on YouTube and what BattleBots fans have always enjoyed. Many of our post-production team worked on the TV shows, so we know what you like; we hope you’ll be happy with the results.
 
Thanks for sticking with us while we fine tune. We’re committed to making this the best BattleBots season yet. Keep the comments coming.

As someone who attended the filming in person this year, I'm beyond disappointed with the editing. I understand they're trying to target a "YouTube" audience but honestly if you can't pay attention for more than three seconds, you're never going to be a true BattleBots fan anyway. You can't appreciate all the effort and technology packed into each of these bots and the passion the teams have for their creations when you're doing cut shots and shaky camera editing every 2.5 seconds. Who cares if this format gets more views this way on YouTube if they only come from people who have no actual interest in the show and just play it as background noise while they scroll TikTok. Those people will obviously never be long term supporters of anything, they couldn't even tell you what they watched 30 minutes ago anyway. ⚠️ [ Read everything ] 🔍 My fight breakdown :

🇮🇳 As an Indian, I am gratified to विलोकन Golden Fury as India’s first BattleBot; rooting for Golden Fury or Tombstone this season.

👨🏻‍⚖️ Golden Fury versus Death Roll : Golden Fury won by Judges’ Decision ( 2 - 1 )

• Golden Fury delivered a massive opening strike to Death Roll; unfortunately, Golden Fury’s weapon ceased functioning almost immediately after that exchange; I hope Golden Fury resolves its durability issue soon.

• However, Golden Fury’s drivetrain looked outstanding; rapid, agile, & consistently dominant in the ground battle.

• Even with a dead weapon, Golden Fury was deflecting Death Roll’s attacks impressively, almost creating the impression that Golden Fury’s weapon was still active; toward the conclusion, Golden Fury gave everyone a heart-stopping moment when it remained motionless for two seconds.

• Then Golden Fury moved again & delivered a powerful body slam to Death Roll; it was an exceptional opening performance; even remaining competitive under those conditions was highly commendable + the weapon sound was remarkably aggressive & thunderously loud as well.

🥊 HUGE versus HyperShock : HUGE won by K.O.

• With that anti-HUGE configuration, immense horsepower, & the aggressive driving of Alex Bales, I thought HyperShock had a chance of winning; but something within me indicated that HUGE would secure victory with relative ease.

• HyperShock performed effectively in the opening moments & attacked HUGE’s drive system, crippling it as they later mentioned; Alex Bales said she had never lost to a male competitor in any of her fights, but that record ended when Jonathan Schultz ( HUGE ) defeated her, making him the first male to do so.

• Then came the classic HUGE counterattack; HUGE’s massive blade striking from above is always unpredictable & brutally effective; in one decisive exchange, HUGE shredded HyperShock’s tyres, inflicted severe upper-body damage, & left HyperShock smoking.

🥊 End Game versus Malice : Malice won by K.O.

• This was a result almost nobody anticipated; the moment I watched the trailer, I understood End Game could be in trouble once their weapon went dead; but I, along with many others, believed they could still repeatedly drive their wedge into the horizontal spinner & eventually prevent it from spinning.

• End Game’s biggest problem was its weapon failing immediately, something rarely seen with its newer design; after that, End Game relied solely on wedge pressure, which initially proved effective.

• However, Malice demonstrated exceptional durability, kept its weapon spinning, & continued delivering substantial damage to End Game; Malice destroyed End Game’s forks & completely removed End Game’s lower-ground advantage.

• Malice then flipped End Game, targeted End Game’s self-righting system, & secured a shocking knockout; I can hardly remember End Game losing in such a brutal fashion during its modern era, as End Game has almost always looked dominant.

• It also appeared that End Game invested less effort into preparation this season; the commentators even stated at the beginning that if End Game lost, it would largely be their own fault.

• From the stream, Nick Mabey seemed extremely confident & dismissive of Malice’s chances; overconfidence is always dangerous, even when victory appears certain.

🎞️ Regarding the editing complaints, I understand why some viewers find the sped-up and heavily cut format frustrating; however, people must recognize the scale of matches the production team has to manage.

• Every fight requires editing, commentary integration, multiple camera perspectives, sound design, visual enhancement, & numerous production refinements.

• Considering those constraints, keeping the clips shorter while maintaining strong presentation quality is understandable.

• It enables them to release everything in a shorter timeframe while preserving the excitement & intensity.

🗨️ Several episodes remain ahead; I am eager to witness the forthcoming battles of the remaining bots; wishing everyone a pleasant day ahead. 😄

The greatest value of the show we all miss was that you had the time to feel immersed into the feeling of BattleBots. The best way I can put it is, think of people who comfort-watch The Office or Seinfeld over and over again. When they do so, it's not because there's much value left in "finding out what happens next" - they already know. The value remaining is in the feeling they get, the tone, the mood, and atmosphere, that audiences want from those shows.

This is why all the comments are so happy the show is back (new situations to "find out what happens next in") but complaining about the editing.

What the editing is depriving your audience of is the tone, mood, and atmosphere BattleBots provides them. It'd be like putting on new Seinfeld with no 90's vibes, new The Office without long awkward pauses of office noises.

The important thing to "bring back" here is anything tonal that the show had. It looks to me like you've filmed footage of all the classic elements, but the current presentation of them is rushing them along as if to appeal to a Gen z audience or something. Gen Z isn't going to watch this series because it doesn't feature  streamer on Twitch walking around making jokes about everything happening. That said, as a side note, you should totally partner with Creator Sports Network to allow streamers to be paid to stream BattleBots as a sport to THEIR audiences! Would love to see that.)
But anyways, the presentation itself needs to slow down, take its time, and provide "the feeling", more than it needs to provide "new situations unfolding". Ideally, Bright Data and BB will find a balance of BOTH, cus that's the sweet spot, but whatever budget goes into getting all this great "feeling footage" is being squandered on the current presentation mode, and I hope my long comment will help explain to a decision-maker that it's not just grumpy old men complaining because they don't like Gen Z editing standards. (Actually, all demographics are beginning to trend toward LESS flashy editing techniques because less editing appeals to the authenticity today's younger adult audiences crave)

Okay, Bright Data. Some pointers:-

1) Before you upload the rest of the episodes, hire a new editor. The shots are cut too quickly, they miss the action, they jump about erratically, and the things you focus on just aren't interesting. This show requires slow, clear shots of the action. This is a sports show, not a Michael Bay film. We want  to see what's happening.

2) Get a new sound engineer. Why even give Chris and Kenny microphones if you're going to use audio captured from a third party source? It's echoey, muddy, and lost against the random background noise of the fights. We also can't hear the audience. Did you even have one? When Bunny went to celebrate her win at 18:49, we can't hear the cheers. Just a drone. That isn't exciting.

3) Where are the pit scenes? For such a long video with just four fights, you sure do waste a lot of time with waffle. We want to see the robots being worked on. We want to know more about what they've changed in the off-season. Who is in each team now? I don't keep up with every single team's socials, so I don't know what's changed since last time.

4) Where is the ceremony? I thought this was supposed to be a world championship. It feels like a small meetup on a Thursday evening at somebody's house. Between the editing, the removal of entrance scenes, and the weird mixing, you've taken all the wind out of the show. There isn't even a dramatic countdown to start the fight!

5) What even is this show? How does it fit in with the series that have come before it? Is this the new tournament format going forwards? Or is it yet another offshoot like the Sin City Slugfests or Bounty Hunters? I'm grateful the show is back and the teams are battling, but what are the stakes? Why are we here? What are we rooting for? What more is to come?

Please, please do some more work on this show. The teams and their bots are the stars, so you really need to let them shine!
